figures.caption: Figure 2: Evolution of the strategy distribution under OFT where the population is initialised with a Dirac at 0.5. The effect of the learning rate is clear: higher values induce additional variance in the population which in turn induces cooperation. Time has been scaled with the learning rate for comparison.
Figure 4: Evolution of the strategy distribution where the population is initialised with X ∼ Beta(3,3) for the 4 rules. The theoretical solution (solid line) matches the simulations (histogram).
Figure 3: Evolution of the strategy distribution where the population is initialised with X ∼ Uni(0, 1) for the 4 rules. The theoretical solution (solid line) matches the simulations (histogram).
Figure 1: Evolution of the strategy distribution where the population is initialised with X ∼ β(2, 2) for the 4 rules. The theoretical solution (solid line) matches the simulations (histogram).
